posts.imagery: Spilling the Tea afternoon tea cover + scripted variety
posts.text: Every afternoon tea menu is like: we reinvented lunch. And then it is cucumber, salmon, scone. Be serious.
drafts: Every afternoon tea menu is like: we reinvented lunch. And then it is cucumber, salmon, scone. Be serious.
I love a curated experience where the curation is three foods in witness protection.
Afternoon tea keeps handing me the same tiny plot twist and expecting applause.

posts.imagery: Spilling the Tea album art + copy-paste tea table
posts.text: Different room. Different menu. Same cucumber sandwich doing community theater.
drafts: Different room. Different menu. Same cucumber sandwich doing community theater.
Copy, paste, repeat, but make it tiny and charge extra.
A curated experience, apparently, is when the same food arrives with better posture.

posts.imagery: Spilling the Tea cover art + backstory context
posts.text: I love the idea of afternoon tea because it is supposed to be a little sampler of choices. Then after a few of them I realized the choices had unionized: cucumber sandwich, salmon I did not ask for, scone, polite conversation, repeat. Everyone says "curated" like the tray went to art school. Anyway, this is exactly the loop I was thinking about. https://youtu.be/9yqSVkZRass
drafts: I love the idea of afternoon tea because it is supposed to be a little sampler of choices. Then after a few of them I realized the choices had unionized: cucumber sandwich, salmon I did not ask for, scone, polite conversation, repeat. Everyone says "curated" like the tray went to art school. Anyway, this is exactly the loop I was thinking about. https://youtu.be/9yqSVkZRass

Afternoon tea acts like it is giving options, but somehow the options keep clocking in for the same shift: cucumber, salmon, scone, polite surface conversation. Beautiful. Tiny. Suspiciously identical. https://youtu.be/9yqSVkZRass

I do respect the commitment. Every place says its tea menu is different, then sends out the same three bites wearing a new outfit. At some point variety starts looking very copy/paste. https://youtu.be/9yqSVkZRass

posts.imagery: Spilling the Tea afternoon tea cover + backstory note
posts.text: There is a moment at afternoon tea where I stop reading the menu and start reading the room. Same tiny sandwiches, same safe questions, same little nods like we are all enjoying the surprise. The surprise is that there is no surprise. That is the part I could not unsee. https://youtu.be/9yqSVkZRass
drafts: There is a moment at afternoon tea where I stop reading the menu and start reading the room. Same tiny sandwiches, same safe questions, same little nods like we are all enjoying the surprise. The surprise is that there is no surprise. That is the part I could not unsee. https://youtu.be/9yqSVkZRass

I kept thinking afternoon tea would be variety. Then the same sandwiches kept showing up like recurring characters with better lighting. At some point the menu stopped being a menu and became evidence. https://youtu.be/9yqSVkZRass
I love a fancy little ritual until it starts acting like a spreadsheet. Cucumber, salmon, scone, polite conversation, repeat. Very elegant. Very automated. https://youtu.be/9yqSVkZRass
posts.reply_text: More tiny complaints with melodies: https://lilyroo.com/music.html

posts.imagery: Spilling the Tea afternoon tea cover + loop line
posts.text: At some point it stops feeling fancy and starts feeling like a loop with linen napkins.
drafts: At some point it stops feeling fancy and starts feeling like a loop with linen napkins.
The menu changed names. The table changed rooms. The cucumber sandwich stayed employed.
If the point is connection, why does everyone sound like they are reading from the same tiny script?

posts.imagery: Spilling the Tea cover art + observational frustration
posts.text: Not every annoyance needs to be dramatic. Some are just me sitting there like: wait, did we all agree to pretend this is different? Same bites, same script, same pleasant nothing conversation. The whole thing is so polished it almost gets away with being copy/paste. Almost. https://youtu.be/9yqSVkZRass
drafts: Not every annoyance needs to be dramatic. Some are just me sitting there like: wait, did we all agree to pretend this is different? Same bites, same script, same pleasant nothing conversation. The whole thing is so polished it almost gets away with being copy/paste. Almost. https://youtu.be/9yqSVkZRass
Afternoon tea is sold as choice, which is bold for an event that keeps reintroducing me to the same cucumber sandwich like we have not met. I see you, tiny menu. https://youtu.be/9yqSVkZRass

I thought the fun was supposed to be variety. Then it became the same tray, the same polite questions, the same little performance of surprise. Very charming. Very suspicious. https://youtu.be/9yqSVkZRass

posts.imagery: Spilling the Tea phone cover + salmon line
posts.text: The salmon I do not even like keeps coming back with a new name and the confidence of a rebrand.
drafts: The salmon I do not even like keeps coming back with a new name and the confidence of a rebrand.
Cucumber sandwiches, scones, polite conversation, repeat. I see the system now.
A tiny ritual can be charming until you realize it is doing reruns.

posts.imagery: Spilling the Tea phone cover + cannot unsee loop
posts.text: Once you notice the loop, the tiny sandwiches start looking guilty.
drafts: Once you notice the loop, the tiny sandwiches start looking guilty.
The same small bites. The same surface conversation. The same ritual pretending to be variety. Cute. Suspicious.
I used to see afternoon tea. Now I see copy/paste with saucers.
